title: Vertical Signal Transmitter
description: Carries a redstone signal straight up or down through multiple floors using a column of redstone dust on solid blocks, or via a torch tower (alternating torch-block pattern) for inverted vertical transmission over many layers. Each torch-block pair = 1 NOT inversion, so use pairs to preserve polarity.

steps.title: Ground-floor input
steps.explanation: Lever at (0,1,0) at ground level. This is the signal source that needs to travel upward.

steps.title: First torch-block pair (level 1→2)
steps.explanation: Solid block at (0,2,0). Redstone torch on its side face at (0,2,0) — the torch is lit when the level below is OFF. When the lever goes ON, it extinguishes this torch. This is the first inversion stage.

steps.title: Second torch-block pair (level 3→4)
steps.explanation: Another solid block at (0,3,0), with a second redstone torch on its side. This second inversion restores the original polarity — lever ON → torch 1 OFF → torch 2 ON. Every two pairs maintains signal polarity.

steps.title: Top-floor output lamp
steps.explanation: Redstone lamp at (0,4,0) on the top floor. Torch 2's output reaches it via the stone block at y=3. With lever ON, lamp is ON (double-inverted = non-inverted). Extend the tower by repeating torch-block pairs — each pair adds one floor.
